title: eryngium bonplandii
intro: Eryngium bonplandii, also known as Sea Holly or Bonpland's Eryngium, is a perennial plant species native to South America, characterized by its thistle-like flowers and silvery-gray foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Eryngium bonplandii include reducing inflammation, relieving coughs, and soothing irritated mucous membranes. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
